draft.introduction: Book 3 of the Ihya addresses the mysteries of purity (tahara), moving from external ritual washing to the inner purification of the heart. Ghazali argues that outward cleanliness is meaningless without corresponding spiritual transformation.

translation.original_ref: Ihya Ulum al-Din, Book 3, Kitab Asrar al-Tahara
translation.text: Know that purity has four degrees. The first is purifying the outward body from impurities and waste. The second is purifying the limbs from crimes and sins. The third is purifying the heart from blameworthy traits and vile character. The fourth is purifying the innermost self (sirr) from everything other than God Most High. This is the purity of the prophets and the saints.

translator_notes.note: The four-degree model reflects Ghazali's method throughout the Ihya: he begins with the external legal requirement, then reveals progressively deeper spiritual dimensions. The fourth degree—purifying the innermost self of everything but God—is essentially the Sufi concept of tawhid applied to the soul.
